wine_restaurants.name: Landhaus Bacher
wine_restaurants.address: Südtiroler Platz 2, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Austrian with Mediterranean influences; Wachau seasonal produce
wine_restaurants.description: Founded by chef Lisl Wagner-Bacher in 1979 and continued by Thomas Dorfer from 2010. Awarded 2 Michelin stars in 2025. Klaus Wagner and daughter Susanne curate an exceptional wine list strong in Wachau, Bordeaux and Burgundy.

verified.address_quoted: Südtiroler Platz 2, 3512 Mautern an der Donau

wine_restaurants.name: Hofmeisterei Hirtzberger
wine_restaurants.address: Hauptstraße 74, 3610 Wösendorf in der Wachau, Austria
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Modern Austrian with Wachau seasonal produce; set menus of 4-7 courses
wine_restaurants.description: Located in a 1320-built monastery building, the Hofmeisterei opened in 2014 as the restaurant arm of Weingut Franz Hirtzberger. Over 2,100 labels cover the wine list, with the Veltliner and champagne sections especially strong.

wine_restaurants.name: Loibnerhof
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Classical Austrian regional with contemporary touches; heuriger-restaurant hybrid
wine_restaurants.description: A 400-year-old vaulted inn in Unterloiben serving Emmerich Knoll wines exclusively. The Biedermeier interior and seasonal garden garden rank among the most picturesque restaurant settings in the Wachau, with a kitchen open Wednesday to Sunday.

wine_restaurants.name: Weinstube Nikolaihof Wachau
wine_restaurants.address: Nikolaigasse 3, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Austrian organic cuisine; biodynamic Demeter-certified ingredients
wine_restaurants.description: Christine Saahs' organic wine restaurant at Austria's oldest winery. Open late April to early November from Wednesday, the kitchen pairs Wachau biodynamic cuisine with Nikolaihof's own estate wines under a century-old tree in the garden.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Schloss Dürnstein
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Fine Austrian with Danube terrace views; seasonal Wachau regional produce
wine_restaurants.description: The restaurant of the only 5-star hotel in Lower Austria, set in a 17th-century palace above the Danube. The terrace is open to non-hotel guests from late March. The historic cellar holds hundreds of different bottles with a strong Wachau focus.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Löwenherz - Hotel Richard Löwenherz
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Austrian regional; Danube fish, seasonal game, Wachau vegetables
wine_restaurants.description: The restaurant of the 4-star Hotel Richard Löwenherz in a former convent on the Danube bank in Dürnstein. Third-generation Thiery family hospitality pairs carefully sourced regional produce with a Wachau-forward wine list.

wine_restaurants.name: Wachauerstube Loiben
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Upscale Austrian tavern; Danube fish, local game, seasonal Wachau produce
wine_restaurants.description: A renovated country inn serving refined tavern cuisine with a wine list built around Tegernseerhof and other Unterloiben producers. The kitchen runs Friday to Tuesday from 11:30 and the quiet garden terrace seats wine-tour cyclists.

wine_restaurants.name: Florianihof
wine_restaurants.address: Wienerstraße 4, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Austrian regional; seasonal menu with Danube fish and local produce
wine_restaurants.description: A well-regarded family restaurant in Mautern near the Danube crossing, serving Austrian classics with an emphasis on local sourcing. The wine list draws on Wachau and Kremstal estates and the kitchen focuses on seasonal availability.

wine_restaurants.name: Landgasthof Schickh
wine_restaurants.address: Klein-Wien 69, 3512 Mautern an der Donau, Austria
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Contemporary Austrian bourgeois; Wachau market produce, duck liver, Danube fish
wine_restaurants.description: One of the most beautiful garden-restaurants in the Wachau, in the hamlet of Klein-Wien near Mautern. Traditional specialties including duck liver terrine, fish soup and veal butter schnitzel, paired with Wachau estate wines.

wine_restaurants.name: Gartenhotel Pfeffel Genuss-Restaurant
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Regional Austrian; own garden produce, Wachau seasonal vegetables and game
wine_restaurants.description: The gourmet restaurant of the family-run Gartenhotel & Weingut Pfeffel, open daily March to December. Own-garden and own-vineyard produce drives the kitchen; wine list drawn exclusively from the estate's Steinfeder, Federspiel and Smaragd range.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Sänger Blondel
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Austrian with seasonal Wachau ingredients; apricot specialties
wine_restaurants.description: The garden restaurant of Hotel Sänger Blondel in Dürnstein, named for the troubadour who found Richard the Lionheart imprisoned in the hilltop fortress. Seasonal specialties include rostbraten, apricot dumplings and strudel, with a Wachau-dominant wine list.

wine_restaurants.name: WeinSpitz Restaurant - Weingut Donabaum
wine_restaurants.address: In der Spitz 3, 3620 Spitz an der Donau, Austria
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Austrian estate cuisine paired with Donabaum Wachau wines; seasonal tasting menus
wine_restaurants.description: The in-house restaurant of Boutiquehotel WeinSpitz, the estate hotel of Weingut Donabaum in Spitz. Exclusive estate wines from the Strawanzer and Setzberg vineyards are poured alongside tasting menus using local Wachau produce.

verified.address_quoted: In der Spitz 3, 3620 Spitz an der Donau
